methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Nucleus Genomics is a Series A biotech company that has raised $32M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of eight private and three public companies in Biotech & Health, including Series B-stage comparables such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next funding round, implies a valuation of $64M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields a median valuation of $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, though three Biotech & Health public companies show a median P/S of 5.8x. The triangulated range across both applicable methods is $54M (low), $434M (median), and $926M (high).

The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects uncertainty in comparable staging and growth trajectories within frontier biotech. The peer set skews toward later-stage, well-capitalized companies (predominantly Series B), which may overstate appropriate valuation for an early Series A company. Key uncertainties include the company's product maturity relative to peers, capital efficiency, and underlying revenue or clinical progress not captured in this analysis.
